s2.q: Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) is defined as:
options: Carbohydrate intolerance of variable severity with onset during pregnancy
s2.explain: GDM is carbohydrate intolerance of variable severity with onset or first recognition during pregnancy.

s2.q: Effects of diabetes on pregnancy include all EXCEPT:
s2.explain: DM in pregnancy is associated with macrosomia (not IUGR), polyhydramnios, congenital anomalies, and preeclampsia.

s2.q: A classic congenital anomaly associated with pregestational DM is:
s2.explain: Sacral agenesis (caudal regression syndrome) is a classic anomaly associated with pregestational diabetes.

s2.q: First-line treatment for gestational diabetes is:
options: Medical nutrition therapy and lifestyle modification
s2.explain: MNT and lifestyle modification with blood glucose monitoring is first-line. Insulin is added if glycemic targets are not met.

s2.q: Which of the following best defines this condition?
options: A pathological deviation from normal anatomy or function
An incidental finding with no clinical significance
s2.explain: A definition describes the essential nature of a condition as a deviation from normal.

s3.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition
It is rarely relevant in clinical practice
It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
s3.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

s3.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings

options: OGTT at 6-12 weeks postpartum and lifestyle counseling
s5.explain: OGTT should be performed at 6-12 weeks postpartum to reclassify glucose tolerance, with lifestyle modification and surveillance.
